page: Amazon Web Services r8ib.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 384 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) derived from 192 physical Intel cores, utilizing hyper-threading with 2.0 threads per core. The processors operate at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. This instance is equipped with 3072.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

The r8ib.96xlarge instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. There are no integrated GPUs on this instance, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its massive memory footprint of over 3 terabytes and high-frequency Intel processors, this instance is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. The high resource density of RAM relative to vCPUs makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for large-scale in-memory databases, big data analytics, and enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems. The lack of local storage and GPU acceleration represents a tradeoff, positioning this server specifically for workloads that rely on network-attached storage and CPU-bound memory operations rather than local disk I/O or parallel GPU processing.
description: Amazon Web Services r8ib.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance designed for large-scale enterprise workloads. Powered by Intel x86_64 processors running at 3.9 GHz, it features 384 dedicated vCPUs across 192 physical cores and 3072.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications, such as in-memory databases, large-scale data analytics, and enterprise resource planning systems, where massive memory capacity and high network throughput are prioritized over local storage and graphics acceleration.

tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring three terabytes of RAM, high-frequency Intel processors, and high-speed network bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
